title_en: She Was Given Up by Her Chinese Parents—and Spent 14 Years Trying to Find a Way Back
title_zh: 她被亲生父母遗弃于中国，在寻找归途的路上坚持了14年

paragraphs.en: A girl is found on a street in Ma’Anshan, China, in May 1993. Her paternal grandfather, the story goes, set her down and walked away. No explanation. It’s unclear how long she’s been outside when somebody arrives and takes her to the orphanage.
paragraphs.zh: 1993年5月，在中国马鞍山的一条街上发现了一名小女孩。根据说法，是她的爷爷（父系）把她放下后离开了。没有留下任何说明。等到有人发现并把她带到孤儿院时，她到底在街头待了多久，至今仍不清楚。

paragraphs.en: A white woman adopts the girl and brings her to America in August 1994. She gives her an English name.
paragraphs.zh: 1994年8月，一位白人女性收养了这个女孩，并将她带到美国。她给女孩取了一个英文名。

paragraphs.en: In spring 2010, when Youxue (her Chinese name) was a high school sophomore in Dallas, Texas, she decided to start searching for her birth parents. She knew it wouldn’t be easy. Given the international nature of her adoption and the under-the-table circumstances in which most Chinese children were relinquished, there was a strong likelihood she would never find them. But her adoptive mother was supportive and found a “searcher” through Yahoo groups, one of the first forums where adoptees connected online. In China, the searcher plastered posters of Youxue and her information in high-traffic areas of Ma’Anshan, in Anhui Province, and went to the police station that was listed in Youxue’s certification of abandonment. There, the searcher was able to access records and find a short note that Youxue had apparently been left with.
paragraphs.zh: 2010年春季，当玉雪（她的中文名）在美国德州达拉斯读高二时，她决定开始寻找生身父母。她知道这不会容易。由于这次收养具有跨国性质，而当时大多数中国弃养儿童都是在暗中、非正式的情况下被交出的，她很可能永远都找不到他们。幸运的是，她的养母表示支持，并通过 Yahoo 群组找到了一个“寻亲人”；这是最早一批收养者在网上互相联系的论坛之一。在中国，这位寻亲人把玉雪的照片和信息贴满了马鞍山（安徽省）人流量大的区域，并前往玉雪《遗弃证明》上登记的警察站。就在那儿，寻亲人得以查阅记录，并找到了一张简短的备注，写着玉雪似乎就是被留在这里的。

paragraphs.en: In September, several families came forward. One of them seemed like a potential match. They had an older daughter and a younger son. Looking at photographs, Youxue thought she could see a resemblance. For the maternity DNA test, she sent off a cotton swab with buccal cells from the inside of her cheek, along with a few strands of hair.
paragraphs.zh: 9月，几个家庭主动联系了她。其中有一个家庭看起来很像是合适人选。他们有一个大女儿和一个小儿子。看着照片时，玉雪觉得她能从中看到相似之处。为了做亲子DNA鉴定，她寄出了一根口腔拭子（取的是口腔内侧颊黏膜细胞）以及几缕头发。

paragraphs.en: Here are 23 ways China is rewiring the future.
paragraphs.zh: 这是中国正在重塑未来的23种方式。

paragraphs.en: In November, she received a text from her adoptive mother that the DNA results had come back positive. There was a match! She wanted to tell all her friends and family; she felt whole. She started taking Mandarin lessons and texting with her birth parents. They said they loved each other and couldn’t wait to meet.
paragraphs.zh: 2017年11月，她收到养母发来的短信，说DNA结果已经出来了。出现了匹配！她想把这件事告诉所有朋友和家人；她觉得自己终于完整了。她开始上中文课，并与生父母用短信交流。他们说自己也很爱她，迫不及待想见面。

paragraphs.en: But when she was on spring break in 2011, Youxue’s birth father told her that her birthday was September 11, 1994. This was impossible. Youxue had already been adopted by then. Thinking it was a mistake, Youxue replied, but he insisted: Mother knows birth date.
paragraphs.zh: 但2011年春假期间，Youxue的生父告诉她，她的生日是1994年9月11日。这完全不可能。到那时Youxue早已被领养了。Youxue以为这是个错误，于是回复说这是搞错了，但对方坚持说：母亲知道你的出生日期。

paragraphs.en: After checking with the DNA company, Youxue found out they had emailed her someone else’s results. This was not her biological family. Devastated, Youxue deleted all of her messages with the family and all of her photographs of them. She knew she would regret it, and that they could even be useful for another adoptee, but she couldn’t bear to hold onto them anymore. To want something is to expose yourself to pain, and choosing to search means opening yourself to heartbreak.
paragraphs.zh: 在与DNA公司核实后，Youxue发现他们把别人的结果发到了她的邮箱。这不是她的生物学亲属。她心碎了，删除了与这家人的所有聊天记录和所有他们的照片。她知道自己会后悔，也知道这些照片可能对其他被领养者有帮助，但她再也承受不了继续留着它们。想要拥有某样东西，就意味着把自己暴露在痛苦之下；选择去寻找，就等于把自己置于心碎的风险中。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, in a small village in China’s Anhui Province, a mother asked her adult daughter and teenage son to help her search for her two relinquished daughters. She had long wanted to look for them, but she spoke only her local dialect and had little access to technology. With no formal education, she didn’t know where to begin, and nobody was sure how to help.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，在中国安徽省一个小村庄里，一位母亲请她已成年的女儿和青少年时期的儿子帮她寻找那对被送走的亲生女儿。她一直想去找她们，但她只会说本地方言，而且几乎没有接触现代技术的机会。由于没有受过正规教育，她不知道该从哪里开始，也没人真正知道该如何帮她。

paragraphs.en: Decades earlier, the conditions that shaped this family’s life were set in motion by China’s one-child policy. The government’s population control program, enacted in the late 1970s, turned family planning into state-mandated decisions about which children were allowed to exist. In the ’80s, rural parents were allowed to have a second child only if the first was a daughter. Families who violated the policy received large fines and other penalties, sometimes sterilization and physical violence.
paragraphs.zh: 几十年前，这个家庭生活轨迹的成因源于中国的计划生育政策。20世纪70年代末实施的国家生育控制方案，把家庭生育决策变成了国家规定的“允许哪些孩子存在”的问题。到了20世纪80年代，农村父母只有在第一个孩子是女儿的情况下才被允许生第二个孩子。违反政策的家庭会被处以高额罚款和其他处罚，有时还包括绝育与身体伤害。

paragraphs.en: Today, there are more than 82,000 Chinese adoptees in the United States, most adopted between 1999 and 2016. More than 60 percent of the children adopted in that period were girls. The majority of adoptive parents are white, wealthy, and well educated. Because child abandonment is illegal in China, very little documentation connects Chinese adoptees with their birth families.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，美国有超过82,000名中国籍被领养人，其中大多数是在1999年至2016年间被收养的。那一时期被收养的儿童中，超过60%是女孩。多数收养父母是白人、富有且受过良好教育。由于在中国弃婴是非法的，因此几乎没有正式文件能把中国被领养人和他们的生父母家庭联系起来。

paragraphs.en: In the summer of 2011, only a few months after the false match, Youxue and her adoptive mother traveled to China to try searching again. Through a friend who had been adopted from the same orphanage and was now reunited with his birth family, they found another searcher who, along with a local radio personality, had helped make a successful reunion in the past. With access to police records and the short note the first searcher found, they finally had more context to move forward.
paragraphs.zh: 2011年夏天，在那次错误匹配发生后仅几个月，优雪和她的养母再次前往中国尝试寻找。通过一位同一收容所出身、如今已与生身家庭重聚的朋友介绍，她们找到了另一位寻找者；这位寻找者与一位当地电台节目主持人一起，在过去帮助过一场成功团聚。借助警方记录以及第一位寻找者找到的那张简短便笺，她们终于有了更多线索可以继续前进。

paragraphs.en: In Ma’Anshan, Youxue did newspaper interviews, online news interviews, and even a television interview that ran on all the local buses. She was searching for families that had relinquished a daughter between August 1993 and January 3, 1994, because her orphanage documents stated she was likely born around that time. She did blood tests. That summer, one family matched everything. Both parents had her blood type. They even knew what was on the note left with the baby; they said they had written that note years earlier in a moment of desperation.
paragraphs.zh: 在马鞍山，友雪做了报纸采访、在线新闻采访，甚至做过一场在当地所有公交车上播出的电视采访。她正在寻找在1993年8月到1994年1月3日之间弃养女儿的家庭，因为她的孤儿院档案显示她大概出生在那个时期。她还做了验血。那个夏天，有一家家庭的资料完全匹配。夫妻两人都和她有同样的血型。他们甚至知道当年抱着婴儿一起留下的纸条上写了什么；他们说当年在绝望的瞬间写下了那张纸条。

paragraphs.en: With a news crew in tow, Youxue and her adoptive mother caravaned to the family’s house in a neighboring township. Nobody could stop crying. But the second Youxue saw the mother step out of her house, she knew something wasn’t right. They looked nothing alike.
paragraphs.zh: 在新闻摄制组陪同下，友雪和她的养母坐车前往那户人家所在的邻近乡镇。没有人能停住眼泪。但当友雪第一次看到那位母亲走出屋门时，她就知道有什么不对劲。他们完全不相像。

paragraphs.en: Youxue had long suspected her paperwork wasn’t accurate. In 1994, when her mother adopted her, she was tall for her age. The documents listed her as a seven-month-old. She’d had developmental delays, common for children in orphanages who are malnourished, but even accounting for those, she was likely older than her papers showed. She’d also had more teeth than a seven-month-old normally would.
paragraphs.zh: 友雪早就怀疑自己的领养手续不准确。1994年，她的养母收养她时，她当时已经高于同龄人了。档案却把她记录为7个月大。她有发育迟缓，这在营养不良的孤儿院孩子中很常见，但即便把这一点都考虑在内，她的实际年龄也很可能比纸面年龄更大。她的牙齿也比一个7个月大的孩子通常会长出的要多。

paragraphs.en: Now she knew: The note, the police records, the photographs—all of it had to be somebody else’s. In 1993, a girl was left on the stoop of a police station in Ma’Anshan, but it wasn’t Youxue. That girl, hopefully still alive, may have Youxue’s papers, because Youxue has hers.
paragraphs.zh: 现在她明白了：那张纸条、警局记录、照片——所有这些，必定都属于别人。1993年，在马鞍山，一名女孩被遗弃在警察局台阶上，但那不是友雪。那位女孩——希望她仍然活着——可能正带着友雪的资料，因为友雪手里拿着自己的那些资料。

paragraphs.en: Youxue continued her search. All that she had known about the circumstances of her birth was incorrect. The only thing she could be certain of was the orphanage her mother had adopted her from.
paragraphs.zh: Youxue 继续了她的寻找。她关于自己出生经历所知道的一切都是错误的。她唯一可以确定的是，母亲是从哪家孤儿院领养了她。

paragraphs.en: In the fall of 2012, Youxue’s new searcher reached out again and said that somebody had seen her footage and wanted her to be on the Chinese TV show Wait for Me, which tries to connect missing children with family members. Later that year, she appeared on the show, the first non-Chinese citizen to be featured. One family eventually came forward, but they were eliminated based on blood type.
paragraphs.zh: 2012年秋天，Youxue 的新调查者再次联系她，表示有人看到了她的视频，并希望她能出现在中国电视节目《Wait for Me》里，该节目试图把走失的孩子与家人重新连结。那一年晚些时候，她登上了节目，成为首位登场的非中国公民。后来有一个家庭站了出来，但由于血型不符，他们被淘汰了。

paragraphs.en: Even though Youxue continued learning Chinese, her hope began to falter. Years passed. By 2020, as Covid-19 outbreaks swept across China, Youxue couldn’t escape the fact that her birth parents were getting older and that perhaps time had run out.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管 Youxue 还在继续学中文，但她的希望开始动摇。岁月流逝。到了2020年，随着新冠肺炎疫情在中国肆虐，Youxue 无法回避一个事实：她的生父母正在变老，也许时间已经不多了。

paragraphs.en: A woman in Anhui Province saw one of Youxue’s online interviews, but she didn’t recognize her. She scrolled past, unaware that the woman on her screen was her niece. Her sister, Youxue’s mother, had been searching for Youxue for a long time.
paragraphs.zh: 安徽省的一位女士看到过 Youxue 的一次在线采访，但她没有认出她。她只是划过那段视频，不知道屏幕上的女子正是她的侄女。Youxue 的母亲，正是那位女士的姐姐，一直在长时间地寻找 Youxue。

paragraphs.en: In recent years, a growing number of Chinese American adoptees have begun looking for their birth families, many for the first time as adults. Empowered by consumer DNA tests like 23andMe and AncestryDNA, they are finding relatives in ways that would have been impossible a decade ago. One woman I talked to located her birth parents within days of uploading her DNA. Another, Alyse, discovered a cousin, also adopted and living in the United States, after finding a 14 percent genetic match. When they first met over FaceTime, the two sat in silence, studying each other’s faces. “Actually seeing each other was really surreal,” Alyse told me. Like many adoptees now rediscovering relatives across continents, their conversation turned from family history to the eerie parallels of their lives. Both were pursuing doctorates; both filled their free time with art: knitting, crocheting, painting. The similarities felt like proof of some phantom thread that had always connected them.
paragraphs.zh: 近年来，越来越多的华裔美国收养者开始寻找自己的生身家庭，许多人是第一次在成年后开始寻找。得益于 23andMe 和 AncestryDNA 等消费级基因检测服务，她们正在以十年前几乎不可能的方式找到亲属。我采访的一位女性在上传 DNA 的几天内就找到了自己的生父母。另一个女孩 Alyse 在发现一个 14% 的遗传匹配后，找到了一个也在美国被收养、与她有血缘关系的表亲。两人第一次在 FaceTime 上见面时，双方都沉默着端详着对方的脸。Alyse 告诉我，“真正见到彼此，真的非常超现实。”和许多如今跨越大洋重拾亲属关系的收养者一样，她们的谈话从家族史转向了双方人生经历中的诡异相似之处。她们都在攻读博士学位，都把业余时间投入到艺术中：编织、钩针、绘画。那些相似之处像是在证明，早已有一条看不见的线始终将她们连接在一起。

paragraphs.en: Every year, technology narrows the distance between international adoptees and their biological families. Created in 2009, China’s National Reunion Database allows anybody to go to a police station and submit their DNA to try to find their family members. In 2016, China launched the Reunion System, which includes mapping technology and assistance from Chinese social media giants like Douyin to post information that could help children and families find each other. Similar to Amber Alert, the Reunion System uses nearly 30 mobile apps and media outlets to disseminate information to peoples’ phones. China also set up free blood collection sites across the country. These new efforts reflect the government’s desire to resolve high-profile abduction cases and demonstrate responsiveness to a crisis that has drawn international scrutiny.
paragraphs.zh: 每年，技术都在缩短国际收养者与其生父母之间的距离。中国于 2009 年建立的国家寻亲数据库允许任何人到警察局提交 DNA，以便寻找家人。2016 年，中国推出了“寻亲系统”，其中包括定位技术，并借助抖音等中国社交媒体巨头发布信息，帮助孩子与家庭互相寻找。与 Amber Alert 类似，寻亲系统使用近 30 种移动应用和媒体平台，将信息传送到人们手机上。中国还在全国范围内设置了免费采血点。这些新举措体现了政府希望解决高调拐卖案件，并对引发国际关注的危机做出回应的意图。

paragraphs.en: In April 2024, Youxue heard about the National Reunion Database during a virtual GED Match Day, an online event that helps people consolidate their DNA files in a single place. The fact that the National Reunion Database was new to Youxue underscores how little-known it remains outside of China. The next month, with instructions from the Nanchang Project, a nonprofit that helps Chinese adoptees find their birth families, Youxue mailed her DNA sample to the database.
paragraphs.zh: 2024 年 4 月，Youxue 在一次线上“GED Match Day”中听说了国家寻亲数据库。那是一个帮助人们将 DNA 文件集中到一个地方的在线活动。Youxue 在活动中得知国家寻亲数据库在中国境外仍知晓度很低，这一点也说明了它在国外并不广为人知。次月，在“南昌项目”的指导下——这是一个帮助中国收养者寻找生身家庭的非营利组织——Youxue把自己的 DNA 样本邮寄到了该数据库。

paragraphs.en: Youxue received confirmation that her DNA had arrived in China on July 14. Forty-eight hours later, a remarkable coincidence: A woman in Anhui Province submitted her DNA to the National Reunion Database too.
paragraphs.zh: Youxue 收到确认消息称她的 DNA 已于 7 月 14 日抵达中国。48 小时后，一个惊人的巧合发生了：一个来自安徽省的女性也向国家寻亲数据库提交了她的 DNA。

paragraphs.en: On August 20, Youxue received a congratulatory email from one of the Nanchang Project volunteers. Youxue had a definitive match with two parents. At first, she couldn’t believe it. The last company had sent her a false match. But matching with both parents offered a new level of certainty.
paragraphs.zh: 8月20日，尤雪收到南昌项目一位志愿者发来的一封祝贺邮件。她与两位父母实现了最终匹配。一开始，她简直不敢相信这一点。上一家机构给她推送过错误的匹配。然而，与两位父母都匹配上，给了她前所未有的确定感。

paragraphs.en: A volunteer at the Nanchang Project told Youxue the names of the parents. The next morning, she received an Instagram message from an account she didn’t recognize. It was a message from a friend of her first cousin in China.
paragraphs.zh: 一位南昌项目的志愿者告诉了尤雪这对父母的名字。第二天早上，她收到了一条来自她不认识的账号的 Instagram 私信。那是来自她在中国一位堂表亲朋友的消息。

paragraphs.en: In April 2025, Youxue and her adoptive mother flew to Shanghai. Her biological older sister met them at the airport with her husband and young child. Earlier, over texts, her sister had asked Youxue to give her child—Youxue’s biological nephew—an English name. Youxue chose Henry. Crying and holding two massive bouquets of flowers, her sister hugged her while her brother-in-law held the sign “Welcome to China.” They spent the next day walking around Shanghai and talking.
paragraphs.zh: 2025年4月，尤雪和她的养母飞到上海。她的亲生姐姐带着丈夫和小孩在机场迎接了她们。更早之前，通过短信，她姐姐曾让尤雪给她的孩子——尤雪的亲生侄子——取一个英文名。尤雪给他取名为亨利。姐姐抱着她、哭着，还抱着两大束鲜花；姐夫则举着“Welcome to China”牌子。接下来的一天，他们在上海逛了很多地方，一直边走边聊。

paragraphs.en: Youxue has two older sisters and one younger brother. Her family kept the eldest daughter and relinquished the second and then the third—Youxue. Nobody has been able to track down the second-eldest daughter; her fate remains unknown. Youxue’s family had been pressured by Youxue’s grandparents to have a son, and the family planning office in their region was strict. Her real birth date is eight months off from what the documents said. Youxue has the same skin and lines across her cheeks as her mother, and she looks just like her brother and father. They have the same smile.
paragraphs.zh: 尤雪有两个姐姐和一个弟弟。她家先留下了长女，后来放弃了第二个孩子，再后来是尤雪。第二个长女至今没有人能找到，她的下落不明。尤雪的家人受到了尤雪祖父母“必须要个儿子”的压力，而她所在地区的计划生育办事处也很严格。她真实的出生日期与证明材料上的日期相差了整整八个月。尤雪在皮肤色泽和脸颊线条上与母亲非常相似，也和哥哥、父亲有几乎一模一样的样子——他们拥有同样的笑容。

paragraphs.en: After a few days in Shanghai, her sister’s family drove them to her family’s village, where her parents still lived. Youxue hadn’t been from Ma’Anshan after all, but a poorer village in nearby Wuhu. Relinquished children were often left in higher-trafficked cities where they had a better chance of being found. Her parents and her brother met them outside a hotel. Her father, red-faced and sobbing, wore a blue polo and her mother a red velour tracksuit. They held each other. They hugged her adoptive mother. Youxue later told me that her father cries more easily than her mother.
paragraphs.zh: 在上海待了几天后，姐姐一家的车把他们送到她父母仍然居住的村子。后来才知道，友雪并不来自马鞍山，而是附近更偏远、更穷的芜湖一个村庄。被“放弃”或送养的孩子常常会被送到人口更多、交通更发达的城市去，因为在那里更容易被人找到。她的父母和哥哥在一家酒店外迎接他们。她的父亲脸色通红、泪流满面，穿着蓝色的POLO衫；母亲穿着红色的绒面运动套装。两个人紧紧抱在一起，也拥抱了她的养母。友雪后来告诉我，她父亲比母亲更容易哭。

paragraphs.en: Cousins and aunts and uncles were all waiting. The family hired caterers and shot fireworks when Youxue walked down the street. They laid out a red carpet and gave her and her adoptive mother red cashmere scarves to wear. The entire village was invited to a celebration. An enormous banner said “Welcome home, daughter!” in Chinese and English gold lettering. Ten tables, 22 dishes. Other people in the village didn’t believe that Youxue had been adopted to America until they saw her white, blond adoptive mother. Her family drove them around the village and showed her the fish and crab fields they used to work in, the water reflecting the milky blue of the sky. Looking at her parents laughing, sitting together on a moped, she could see what their young love might have looked like.
paragraphs.zh: 表亲、姑姑、叔叔都在等候着。那天家里请了酒店式的餐饮服务，还在她走上村头时放了鞭炮。大家铺了一条红地毯，还给友雪和她的养母送上了红色羊绒围巾。全村都被邀请去参加这个庆祝会。一个巨大的横幅上用中英文金色大字写着：“欢迎回家，女儿！” 一共摆了十张桌子、二十二道菜。直到看到她白皙、金发的养母，村里一些人仍不敢相信友雪竟被人养到美国去。她的家人开车带着她绕着村子转，向她展示过去她曾经帮着干活过的鱼塘和蟹塘，水面映着乳白色的天光。看到她的父母并肩坐在电动三轮车上笑着，她能想起他们年轻时的爱情也许曾经是这样的样子。

paragraphs.en: One of Youxue’s aunts couldn’t stop crying. She had also relinquished two daughters years ago and desperately wanted to find them.
paragraphs.zh: 友雪的一位姨妈一直哭个不停。多年前她也曾把两位女儿送了出去，如今她几乎不顾一切地想把两个孩子找回来。

paragraphs.en: Youxue’s parents largely spoke the local dialect, although her mother had learned some Mandarin. They worked as farmers for most of their lives and had little opportunity for schooling. Even though Youxue speaks Mandarin, she needed a translator to communicate with them.
paragraphs.zh: 友雪的父母大多只会说当地方言，尽管她母亲会一点普通话。夫妻俩大半生都是做农活的，受教育机会很少。尽管友雪自己会说普通话，她仍然需要翻译才能和父母顺畅沟通。

paragraphs.en: Most Chinese families didn’t even have access to clean water until the 2000s, and many lived in abject poverty. “We’re so sorry.” That was what everyone in her biological family told her. Her parents, her aunts and uncles, her siblings. But Youxue told me that “forgive” wasn’t the right word for how she felt about the circumstances of her relinquishment. She doesn’t blame them: “I don’t grieve for just myself. I grieve for them too.”
paragraphs.zh: 直到2000年代，许多中国家庭连干净的水都拿不到，生活在极端贫困之中。 “我们很抱歉。”这就是她生母生父、姑姑、叔叔和兄弟姐妹告诉她的话。可尧雪（音译）告诉我，“原谅”这个词并不能准确表达她对自己被放弃境遇的感受。她并不把过错归咎于他们：“我不只为自己悲伤。我也为他们悲伤。”

paragraphs.en: Every year, more Chinese adoptees send off DNA kits, upload photographs, or submit their DNA to the National Reunion Database. As databases grow and social networks interconnect, the chance of reunion grows. Chinese police now use not only DNA analysis but also face recognition to help families reunite. Some adoptees post their stories on RedNote, a social media platform similar to TikTok. What once felt like an impossible quest now feels like a movement. For Youxue and thousands of others, every reunion proves that the past is not sealed off forever.
paragraphs.zh: 每年，越来越多的中国收养者寄出DNA检测试剂盒、上传照片，或把自己的DNA提交到“国家寻亲数据库”。随着数据库不断扩大和社交网络互联，重聚的机会也在增加。中国警方如今不仅使用DNA分析，还使用人脸识别帮助家庭团聚。许多收养者会在“红笔记”上分享自己的故事，这个社交平台与TikTok类似。曾经看似不可能完成的寻找，如今更像一场运动。对尧雪和成千上万的人来说，每一次重逢都证明：过去并非永远封存。

paragraphs.en: After 14 years of searching, three on the ground in China, Youxue is now in regular contact with her birth family. When she talks about her story, her face brightens. She’ll be back in Wuhu in February to spend Chinese New Year with them. “Even before I met my biological family, I loved them,” she says. “I’ve loved them throughout this entire process.” Youxue now runs a nonprofit that creates online groups and in-person meetups for adoptees. In addition to her missing older sister, she is still searching for two female cousins.
paragraphs.zh: 经过14年的寻找，在中国实地走访了三年后，尧雪如今与生身家庭已恢复了长期稳定的联系。每当她讲起自己的经历，脸上都显得格外明亮。她说：“即使在真正见到我的生身家庭之前，我就已经爱上了他们。” “在这整个过程中，我始终都爱着他们。”现在，尧雪运营一个非营利组织，为收养者搭建线上群组和线下见面会。除了正在寻找失散多年的姐姐，她还在继续寻找两位表姐妹。
